Concurrency Patterns

Last updated: Apr 11, 2026

Table of Contents

  • Overview
  • When to Use
  • Pattern 1: Print in Order / Sequence Control
  • Using CountDownLatch
  • Using Semaphore
  • Pattern 2: Producer-Consumer / Bounded Buffer
  • Pattern 3: Read-Write Lock / H2O Problem
  • Key Concurrency Primitives
  • LC Example

Concurrency Patterns (Java)

Overview

Google sometimes asks concurrency/threading problems at L4+. These test understanding of synchronization, thread safety, and concurrent data structure design.

When to Use

  • Problem explicitly mentions threads, parallel execution, or producer/consumer
  • Design problems requiring thread-safe data structures

Pattern 1: Print in Order / Sequence Control

Using CountDownLatch

1// LC 1114 — Print in Order
2class Foo {
3    private CountDownLatch latch1 = new CountDownLatch(1);
4    private CountDownLatch latch2 = new CountDownLatch(1);
5
6    public void first(Runnable printFirst) throws InterruptedException {
7        printFirst.run();
8        latch1.countDown();
9    }
10
11    public void second(Runnable printSecond) throws InterruptedException {
12        latch1.await();
13        printSecond.run();
14        latch2.countDown();
15    }
16
17    public void third(Runnable printThird) throws InterruptedException {
18        latch2.await();
19        printThird.run();
20    }
21}

Using Semaphore

1// LC 1115 — Print FooBar Alternately
2class FooBar {
3    private int n;
4    private Semaphore fooSem = new Semaphore(1);
5    private Semaphore barSem = new Semaphore(0);
6
7    public FooBar(int n) { this.n = n; }
8
9    public void foo(Runnable printFoo) throws InterruptedException {
10        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
11            fooSem.acquire();
12            printFoo.run();
13            barSem.release();
14        }
15    }
16
17    public void bar(Runnable printBar) throws InterruptedException {
18        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
19            barSem.acquire();
20            printBar.run();
21            fooSem.release();
22        }
23    }
24}

Pattern 2: Producer-Consumer / Bounded Buffer

1// LC 1188 — Design Bounded Blocking Queue
2class BoundedBlockingQueue {
3    private Queue<Integer> queue = new LinkedList<>();
4    private int capacity;
5    private ReentrantLock lock = new ReentrantLock();
6    private Condition notFull = lock.newCondition();
7    private Condition notEmpty = lock.newCondition();
8
9    public BoundedBlockingQueue(int capacity) { this.capacity = capacity; }
10
11    public void enqueue(int element) throws InterruptedException {
12        lock.lock();
13        try {
14            while (queue.size() == capacity) notFull.await();
15            queue.offer(element);
16            notEmpty.signal();
17        } finally { lock.unlock(); }
18    }
19
20    public int dequeue() throws InterruptedException {
21        lock.lock();
22        try {
23            while (queue.isEmpty()) notEmpty.await();
24            int val = queue.poll();
25            notFull.signal();
26            return val;
27        } finally { lock.unlock(); }
28    }
29
30    public int size() { lock.lock(); try { return queue.size(); } finally { lock.unlock(); } }
31}

Pattern 3: Read-Write Lock / H2O Problem

1// LC 1117 — Building H2O
2class H2O {
3    private Semaphore hSem = new Semaphore(2);
4    private Semaphore oSem = new Semaphore(0);
5    private CyclicBarrier barrier = new CyclicBarrier(3, () -> {
6        hSem.release(2);
7    });
8
9    public void hydrogen(Runnable releaseHydrogen) throws InterruptedException {
10        hSem.acquire();
11        releaseHydrogen.run();
12        try { barrier.await(); } catch (BrokenBarrierException e) {}
13    }
14
15    public void oxygen(Runnable releaseOxygen) throws InterruptedException {
16        oSem.acquire();  // wait for 2 H first — actually, simpler approach below
17        releaseOxygen.run();
18        try { barrier.await(); } catch (BrokenBarrierException e) {}
19    }
20}

Key Concurrency Primitives

Primitive Purpose Key Methods
synchronized Mutual exclusion wait(), notify(), notifyAll()
ReentrantLock Explicit lock with conditions lock(), unlock(), newCondition()
Semaphore Counting permits acquire(), release()
CountDownLatch One-time gate (count to 0) await(), countDown()
CyclicBarrier Reusable rendezvous point await()
volatile Visibility guarantee —
AtomicInteger Lock-free counter incrementAndGet(), compareAndSet()

LC Example

# Problem Key Concept
1114 Print in Order CountDownLatch / Semaphore
1115 Print FooBar Alternately Semaphore pair
1116 Print Zero Even Odd Semaphore coordination
1117 Building H2O CyclicBarrier + Semaphore
1188 Bounded Blocking Queue ReentrantLock + Condition
1195 Fizz Buzz Multithreaded Semaphore / CyclicBarrier
1226 The Dining Philosophers Deadlock avoidance
